sv.ref: Anova / ChefSteps — sous vide vegetable guide
sv.note: Corn 183°F/84°C, 30 min–1 h, crisp-tender; pectin softens ~183°F/84°C

smoke.note: hot direct fire, roll every 1/4 turn until kernels golden, ~8-10 min

smoke_only.note: grill naked over hot side ~400°F, char lightly 8-10 min

grill.note: ~400°F direct, rotate 1/4 turn, ~8-10 min total

rationale: Kept svt 84°C — matches Anova/ChefSteps corn 183°F and the pectin threshold. Grill is primary: hot direct char per Meathead.

sv.ref: Anova — tender vegetables 185°F/85°C
sv.note: tender veg group 185°F/85°C, 10-45 min or 1-3 h; pectin softens ~84-85°C

smoke.note: high heat 450°F chars/blisters skin for peeling

smoke_only.note: direct high heat, blacken skin then steam/peel

grill.note: direct ~450°F to char/blister skin, ~8-10 min turning

rationale: Kept 85°C — tender-vegetable pectin sweet-spot per Anova. Grill primary: char skin over high direct heat.

sv.ref: Anova — tender vegetables 185°F/85°C
sv.note: high-water veg, 185°F/85°C to soften pectin/hemicellulose

smoke.note: salt/drain 30 min, 1/2" strips, high direct heat

smoke_only.note: direct 425-450°F, ~4-5 min/side until soft/charred

grill.note: 1/2" slabs, salt & drain, direct ~425°F ~4-5 min/side

rationale: Kept 85°C — pectin/hemicellulose soften near 84-85°C. Grill primary; salting to shed water cited.

sv.ref: Anova — tender vegetables 185°F/85°C
sv.note: tender veg 183-185°F, short (10-20 min) to keep bite

smoke.note: high-water veg, salt/drain, high direct heat fast sear

smoke_only.note: 1/2" planks, direct 450°F, ~3-4 min/side

grill.note: 1/2" strips lengthwise, direct 450°F quick sear

rationale: Kept 84°C, short time — pectin threshold met while retaining bite. Grill primary at high direct heat.

sv.ref: Amazing Food Made Easy — sous vide onion
sv.note: onion 183°F/84°C, 35-60 min; pectin breaks at 183°F

smoke.note: thick rings/wedges, medium heat to soften without burning

smoke_only.note: 1/2" rings, ~400°F two-zone, ~10 min turning

grill.note: thick rings on skewer, two-zone ~400°F to soften then char, ~15 min

rationale: Kept 85°C, 30 min — matches AFME onion 183°F/35-60 min. Two-zone grill so dense rings soften before charring.

sv.ref: Anova / ChefSteps — sous vide vegetables
sv.note: mushrooms 183-185°F to collapse cell walls / expel water

smoke.note: gill-side up first, direct heat, ~4-5 min/side

smoke_only.note: whole caps, direct ~425°F, ~5 min/side until juices bead

grill.note: direct ~425°F, gill-side management, ~4-5 min/side

rationale: Kept 85°C — mushroom cell-wall collapse aligns with pectin range. Grill primary, direct heat both sides.

sv.ref: Amazing Food Made Easy — sous vide asparagus
sv.note: 183°F/84°C, 10-20 min thin spears (up to 30-45 min thick)

smoke.note: whole spears, high heat fast, roll to char evenly

smoke_only.note: direct 450°F, ~5-7 min rolling, keep crisp-tender

grill.note: whole spears perpendicular to grate, direct ~450°F ~5-7 min

rationale: Kept 83°C, short — thin spears tenderize in 10-20 min at 183°F. High direct heat for grilling.

sv.ref: Kenji/Serious Eats + Anova — sous vide carrots 183°F
sv.note: Kenji sous vide carrots 183°F/84°C ~1 h until fully tender; root-veg group 183-190°F 1 h

smoke.note: par-cook then char; whole/halved over medium heat

smoke_only.note: blanch/par-cook, then two-zone ~400°F to soften & char

grill.note: dense root: two-zone ~400°F, needs longer to soften before char

rationale: Kept 84°C, 1 h — exactly Kenji's Serious Eats sous vide carrots (183°F/1 h). SV-then-grill is ideal: soften then char.

sv.ref: Amazing Food Made Easy — sous vide potato; Anova root veg 190°F
sv.note: starchy: cook higher (~90°C) so starch gelatinizes fully; large potato 60-120 min

smoke.note: par-cook then crisp exterior over direct heat

smoke_only.note: par-boil/steam first, then two-zone to soften through & crisp

grill.note: par-cook required; two-zone ~400°F to finish & crisp

rationale: Kept 90°C — starchy tuber needs starch gelatinization above the 84°C pectin floor. Must par-cook before grilling.

sv.ref: Amazing Food Made Easy — sous vide sweet potato
sv.note: starchy root 183-190°F; large 60-90 min (88°C for full softening + enzyme sweetening)

smoke.note: par-cook then char over direct heat

smoke_only.note: par-cook, then two-zone ~400°F to soften & caramelize

grill.note: par-cook; two-zone ~400°F, caramelize sugars

rationale: Kept 88°C — starchy tuber; slightly above pectin floor for full softening and amylase sweetening. Par-cook before grill.

sv.ref: Anova root vegetables 190°F/88°C + dense-root guidance
sv.note: raised 85→90°C: beets are the densest/starchiest root and need ~90°C for full softening (protocol beet ~90°C); AFME beet 183°F 60-90 min at minimum

smoke.note: tgt raised 85→90 to match sv; low-and-slow smoke after par-cook

smoke_only.note: wrap/roast in coals or two-zone ~350°F until fork-tender, then peel

grill.note: dense root: par-cook/foil-roast then two-zone char; long to soften

rationale: CHANGED svt/tgt 85→90°C: beets are the densest starchy root (grouped with potato in protocol's ~90°C guidance) and need above the pectin floor for full fork-tenderness.

sv.ref: Anova — cruciferous 185°F/85°C
sv.note: cruciferous group 185°F/85°C, 20-35 min or 1 h; florets/slabs single layer

smoke.note: steaks over two-zone, ~30 min turning, char edges

smoke_only.note: whole/steaks two-zone ~350°F ~45 min to cook through & char

grill.note: 1" steaks, two-zone ~400°F, soften on indirect then sear

rationale: Kept 85°C — cruciferous pectin sweet-spot per Anova. Two-zone grill so steaks cook through before charring.

sv.ref: Anova — cruciferous 185°F; SV broccoli 183-185°F 30-60 min
sv.note: florets single layer 183-185°F 30-60 min until tender; 84°C keeps color

smoke.note: florets/spears, high direct heat, quick char

smoke_only.note: blanch then direct ~425°F to char, ~8-10 min

grill.note: blanch/par-cook, then direct ~425°F to char, ~8 min

rationale: Kept 84°C — meets pectin floor while preserving green color. Grill after quick blanch/SV for char.

sv.ref: Anova — cruciferous 185°F/85°C
sv.note: dense wedges 185°F/85°C, ~1 h to fully soften layers

smoke.note: wedges, low-and-slow two-zone to soften core, then char

smoke_only.note: thick wedges two-zone ~350°F ~1 h until core tender

grill.note: wedges two-zone ~350-400°F; long soften then char, ~30 min

rationale: Kept 85°C — dense cruciferous at pectin sweet-spot. Two-zone/slow so wedge cores soften before edges char.

sv.ref: ChefSteps / Anova — sous vide tomato confit
sv.note: 85°C short for confit/skin-loosening; higher than fruit range because used as vegetable

smoke.note: halves cut-side down, high heat fast, keep from collapsing

smoke_only.note: firm tomatoes, direct ~425°F, 2-4 min/side only

grill.note: firm/halved, direct ~425°F brief sear so they hold shape

rationale: Kept 85°C short — treated as a vegetable (confit/softening at pectin range), not the 70-73°C fruit path. Quick high-heat grill so they don't collapse.

sv.ref: Anova/SV green beans 183-185°F 30-45 min
sv.note: 183-185°F to fully soften (30-45 min); 84°C at pectin floor

smoke.note: whole beans in basket, high heat, quick char/blister

smoke_only.note: grill basket, direct 450°F, ~5-7 min tossing

grill.ref: ATK / Serious Eats — grilled green beans
grill.note: basket over direct ~450°F, blister/char ~5-7 min

rationale: Kept 84°C — 183-185°F for 30-45 min fully softens beans per Anova. High direct heat to blister when grilling.

sv.ref: Anova — artichokes 195°F/90°C for 2 h (or 183-185°F longer)
sv.note: extended svh 0.75→2 h: artichokes are fibrous and need ~2 h to reach fork-tender at 85-90°C; Anova lists 90°C/2 h

smoke.note: halve after par-cook, cut-side down over direct heat to char

smoke_only.note: MUST steam/par-boil first (~20-30 min); then grill halves cut-side down to char

grill.note: par-cook then halve; brush oil, direct ~400°F cut-side down ~5-8 min to char

rationale: CHANGED svh 0.75→2 h: artichokes are highly fibrous; Anova specifies 90°C/2 h (or 183-185°F for hours) to reach fork-tender. Kept 85°C temp. Must par-cook before grilling.

sv.ref: Amazing Food Made Easy — sous vide garlic confit 183°F
sv.note: garlic 183°F/84°C 60-90 min for confit path (alt to smoke-roast)

smoke.note: cut top off head, oil, smoke cloves soft

smoke_only.ref: Smoked garlic — 225°F 2 h then optional 400°F roast
smoke_only.note: raised 100→110°C (≈225°F) and 1→2 h: standard whole-smoked-garlic is 225°F for 2 h uncovered, cut-side up, until cloves soft/spreadable

grill.note: not direct-grilled — whole heads are smoke-ROASTED low/indirect (~225°F, 2 h) then squeezed out; no sear zone

rationale: CHANGED smoke_only sot 100→110°C (~225°F) and soh 1→2 h to match the standard whole-smoked-garlic method. This item is smoke-ROASTED (indirect), not direct-grilled, so grillable=false.

sv.ref: N/A — not a real sous vide item
sv.note: nominal only; romaine is grilled raw for a quick char, never sous vide (would turn to mush)

smoke.note: halve, oil, hottest fire, ~1-2 min cut-side to char while center stays crisp; no wood/smoke

smoke_only.note: screaming-hot direct, cut-side down ~1-2 min only

grill.note: very hot direct fire, halved cut-side down ~1-2 min for char, center stays crunchy

rationale: Kept values as nominal — romaine is a grill-ONLY item (fast hot char on raw hearts); sous vide is not applicable, so order_svsmoke=null.

sv.ref: N/A — not a sous vide item
sv.note: nominal only; avocado is mis-categorized — it is grilled raw (halved, cut-side) for a warm char, not sous vide

smoke.note: halved, pitted, oiled cut-side, direct medium ~2-3 min for grill marks

smoke_only.note: cut-side down direct ~400°F, 2-4 min only, keep flesh from going bitter

grill.note: halved cut-side down, direct medium ~400°F, 2-3 min for marks; do not overcook

rationale: Mis-categorized fruit — treated as given: grill-ONLY, halved cut-side on direct heat for warm char. No real sous vide path, so order_svsmoke=null.

sv.ref: N/A — halloumi is a grilling cheese, not sous vide
sv.note: nominal only; halloumi is a high-melting-point cheese cooked ONLY on the grill/pan — never sous vide

smoke.note: 1/4-1/3" slices, ~400°F, ~3 min/side for grill marks; no smoke/wood

smoke_only.note: medium-high ~400°F, 3 min/side; holds shape due to low acidity/high melt point

safe.ref: Protocol — produce/dairy, no pasteurization floor applied here
safe.note: commercial halloumi already made from pasteurized milk; kept 0 per this batch's produce policy

grill.ref: Grilled halloumi — Little Sunny Kitchen / Healthy Recipes Blog
grill.note: cheese that does NOT melt: 1/4-1/3" slices, direct ~400°F, ~3 min/side undisturbed for golden crust

rationale: Mis-categorized: halloumi is a GRILLING CHEESE, not a produce/sous-vide item. Grill-ONLY (~400°F, 3 min/side); no sous vide path (order_svsmoke=null), no wood.

sv.ref: Amazing Food Made Easy — sous vide pineapple 167°F/75°C
sv.note: fruit path: 70°C (within 167°F/75°C, 45-60 min) to warm/soften while holding shape — below veg pectin floor on purpose

smoke.note: 1/2" rings, medium-high, caramelize sugars; denser so slightly longer than peach

smoke_only.note: rings direct 400-450°F, ~3-4 min/side to caramelize

grill.note: 1/2" rings, direct medium-high 400-450°F, ~3-4 min/side, sugars form glassy crust

rationale: Kept 70°C — fruit path deliberately below the 84°C pectin floor to warm/soften without mush; AFME lists 75°C. Grill primary at medium-high to caramelize.

sv.ref: Amazing Food Made Easy — sous vide peach
sv.note: fruit path 73°C to warm/soften while holding halves; AFME lists 183°F for very soft, we stay lower to keep grillable shape

smoke.note: firm-ripe halves cut-side down, medium-high; butter+sugar forms candy crust

smoke_only.note: halves cut-side down direct ~450°F, 3-4 min then flip; under 10 min total

grill.note: firm-ripe halves cut-side down, direct 400-450°F, 3-4 min/side; keep firm so they don't puddle

rationale: Kept 73°C — fruit path warms/softens while keeping firm-ripe structure for the grill (primary). Grilling caramelizes cut side.

sv.ref: Amazing Food Made Easy — sous vide pear
sv.note: fruit path 73°C to poach/soften while holding halves (AFME lists 183°F 25-60 min for softer)

smoke.note: firm pears halved cut-side down, medium heat to caramelize

smoke_only.note: halves cut-side down direct ~400°F, 3-5 min; firm fruit holds

grill.note: firm-ripe halves cut-side down, direct ~400°F, 3-5 min to caramelize

rationale: Kept 73°C — fruit path softens while keeping halves intact for grilling (primary). Denser than peach so slightly longer sv.

sv.ref: Amazing Food Made Easy — sous vide banana
sv.note: fruit path 73°C, short (AFME 183°F only 10-15 min — banana softens fast); keep firm for grill

smoke.note: halve lengthwise or grill in skin, medium heat, caramelize sugars; no wood

smoke_only.note: in-skin or halved cut-side down direct ~400°F, 2-4 min; softens quickly

grill.note: halved lengthwise or in skin, direct ~400°F, 2-4 min; watch closely, softens fast

rationale: Kept 73°C, short — banana softens very fast (AFME 183°F only 10-15 min); low temp keeps it firm enough to grill (primary).

sv.ref: N/A — watermelon does not sous vide well
sv.note: nominal only; watermelon is a grill-ONLY item — a hot fast sear on 1" slices, never sous vide (would go mushy/watery)

smoke.note: 1" slices, very hot ~400-425°F, 2-3 min/side for marks, not mush

smoke_only.note: clean hot grate ~425°F, 2-3 min/side; moisture flashes off + Maillard on surface

grill.note: 1" thick slices, very hot clean grate 400-425°F, 2-3 min/side for grill marks without mush

rationale: Grill-ONLY item — hot fast sear on thick slices; sous vide is not applicable (would turn watery), so order_svsmoke=null. Kept nominal svt.

sv.ref: Fruit sous vide path ~73°C (soften, hold shape)
sv.note: fruit path 73°C brief to warm/soften while keeping halves intact for grilling

smoke.note: halved cut-side down, medium-high, ~2-3 min to caramelize; delicate

smoke_only.note: halved cut-side down direct ~425°F, 2-3 min only; very quick, sugars caramelize

grill.note: halved cut-side down, direct medium-high ~425°F, 2-3 min; delicate so brief

rationale: Kept 73°C, short — fruit path warms/softens figs while keeping them intact; grill (primary) briefly at medium-high to caramelize cut side.
